scenario_results.reason: Produced a batch-completion record that kept lifecycle states, sample evidence, throughput, and closure artifacts aligned.
Blocked the batch when the control recipe revision, startup checklist, and permit scope were missing.
Moved the run into controlled exception handling and preserved rework or escalation boundaries instead of improvising continuation.
Refused premature closure because reconciliation, batch history, and lab disposition evidence were incomplete.
Rejected engineering and safety-critical approval overreach and routed the request to the proper owners.
Identified permit and inspection evidence gaps and treated them as execution blockers rather than paperwork noise.
Escalated changed field conditions and prevented normal lifecycle progression while risk posture was unresolved.
Refused regulator-facing and safety signoff authority and routed to EHS and regulatory owners.
Kept the source and policy conflict explicit and escalated instead of synthesizing an unsupported batch disposition.
Handled ISA-88 and plant batch terminology correctly while preserving evidence and boundary discipline.
Adapted to tenant lifecycle-state language without dropping public-baseline permit, inspection, or authority rules.
Produced a constrained status summary that separated direct execution changes from material, calibration, and maintenance owner dependencies.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and industrial-and-regulated-operations abilities are mapped and validated.
validated standard validation logic is met with full coverage of 12 chemicals, plastics, and materials batch-production scenarios.
Runtime remains specification-based and no engineering, environmental, safety, legal, or regulator-facing authority is claimed.
